seoTitle: How to Connect PayPal to Binance via P2P (2026)
metaDescription: Direct PayPal deposits aren't supported on Binance, but here's how to buy crypto using PayPal through Binance's secure P2P marketplace instead.
intro: Because financial gateways restrict direct wallet deposits into centralized exchanges, you can't link PayPal directly to a Binance account. This guide shows how to use Binance's P2P marketplace to buy crypto with PayPal through a verified merchant and escrow system instead. It's for anyone looking to bridge PayPal funds into crypto.

steps.heading: Open Binance P2P
steps.body: Log in to Binance, hover over Trade in the top menu, and select P2P.

steps.heading: Filter for PayPal payments
steps.body: Open the Payment dropdown, search for PayPal, select it, and set your target fiat currency.

steps.heading: Choose a verified merchant
steps.body: Review the seller rows and pick one with a high completion rate.

steps.heading: Start the trade
steps.body: Click Buy USDT, enter your amount, then click Buy to trigger the escrow hold that locks the seller's crypto.

steps.heading: Send payment and confirm
steps.body: Copy the recipient's email, send a Goods and Services PayPal payment for that exact amount, then return to Binance and click Transferred, Notify Seller.

faq.question: Why can't I just deposit PayPal directly into Binance?
faq.answer: Major exchanges impose restrictions on direct digital wallet deposits due to risk controls, so P2P trading is the standard workaround.

faq.question: Is the P2P escrow system safe?
faq.answer: Yes, the seller's crypto is locked in escrow until you confirm payment, which protects both sides of the trade.

faq.question: Should I use Goods and Services for the PayPal payment?
faq.answer: Yes, using Goods and Services keeps buyer protection active in case of a payment dispute with the merchant.

faq.question: What if the seller doesn't release the crypto?
faq.answer: Keep clear chat logs of your payment confirmation, since Binance support can review disputes if a seller stalls.
